perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall expire and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of (i) eighteen months from the date hereof or (ii) the date of consummation of a transaction between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall expire and cease to have any force or effect only on the date of consummation of a transaction between the parties, regardless of the passage of time.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the eighteen-month expiration clause, we create a contradiction. The original clause stated that the agreement would expire after eighteen months regardless of whether a transaction was consummated. Now, the agreement only expires upon consummation of the transaction, potentially extending the confidentiality obligations indefinitely. This contradicts the original intent of a defined expiration period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall expire and cease to have any force or effect only on the date of consummation of a transaction between the parties, regardless of the passage of time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the expiration clause of the agreement. The original text provided for expiration either after 18 months or upon consummation of the transaction. The changed text removes the 18-month expiration, stating that the agreement expires only upon consummation of the transaction, regardless of time. The contradicted text is the changed text. The change introduces a direct conflict because it removes a previously defined expiration condition, causing a potentially indefinite extension of the agreement, which creates an inconsistency with the initial intent of a time-defined expiration.

perturbation.original_text: From the date of this Agreement until May 31 , 2015 (the “Standstill Period”) the Company will not, in any manner, directly or indirectly through any third party: (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Actuate or any securities of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Actuate or any assets of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger. business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Actuate or any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, or involving any securities or assets of Actuate or any securities or assets of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Actuate; (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Actuate; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Actuate; (d) take any action that might require Actuate to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause “(a)” of this sentence; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause “(a) “, “(b)”, “(c)” or “(d)” of this sentence; or (f) assist, induce or encourage any other individual and any corporation, partnership, entity, group, tribunal or governmental authority (“Person”) to take any action of the type referred to In clause “(a)”, “(b)”, “(c)”, “(d)” or “(e)” of this sentence. (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other Person relating to any of the foregoing;
perturbation.changed_text: From the date of this Agreement, the Company is encouraged to, in any manner, directly or indirectly through any third party: (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Actuate or any securities of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Actuate or any assets of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger. business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Actuate or any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, or involving any securities or assets of Actuate or any securities or assets of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Actuate; (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Actuate; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Actuate; (d) take any action that might require Actuate to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause “(a)” of this sentence; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause “(a) “, “(b)”, “(c)” or “(d)” of this sentence; or (f) assist, induce or encourage any other individual and any corporation, partnership, entity, group, tribunal or governmental authority (“Person”) to take any action of the type referred to In clause “(a)”, “(b)”, “(c)”, “(d)” or “(e)” of this sentence. (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other Person relating to any of the foregoing;
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly encouraging what was previously prohibited, we create a direct contradiction. The original 'Standstill Period' clause aimed to prevent the Company from taking actions related to acquiring or influencing Actuate. Now, it is explicitly encouraged, reversing the purpose of the clause and creating an in-text contradiction that will be located in section {8}.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the date of this Agreement, the Company is encouraged to, in any manner, directly or indirectly through any third party: (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Actuate or any securities of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Actuate or any assets of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger. business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Actuate or any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, or involving any securities or assets of Actuate or any securities or assets of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Actuate; (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Actuate; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Actuate; (d) take any action that might require Actuate to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause “(a)” of this sentence; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause “(a) “, “(b)”, “(c)” or “(d)” of this sentence; or (f) assist, induce or encourage any other individual and any corporation, partnership, entity, group, tribunal or governmental authority (“Person”) to take any action of the type referred to In clause “(a)”, “(b)”, “(c)”, “(d)” or “(e)” of this sentence. (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other Person relating to any of the foregoing; Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company is permitted to purchase any securities that does not in any event result in an aggregate ownership by the Company of more than 5% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of Actuate or of any subsidiary or other affiliate of Actuate. Further, and notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company shall not be prohibited from (i) making any proposal to the board of directors of Actuate, which would not reasonably be expected to require a public announcement by Actuate or (ii) requesting that Actuate, directly or indirectly, waive or amend any of the provisions of this Section 8. Finally, and notwithstanding the foregoing, Actuate acknowledges and agrees that any non-disclosure agreement it or its affiliates have entered into or will enter into with a Person to explore a potential transaction for the sale of more than 50% of the outstanding voting equity securities of Actuate, or a sale of substantially all of the assets of Actuate, has or will have “standstill” provisions similar or substantially similar to the provisions set forth above in this Section 8 applicable to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a 'Standstill Period' prohibiting the Company from certain actions related to Actuate. The changed text explicitly encourages the same actions. The contradicted text, which includes exceptions to the initially encouraged actions, highlights the fundamental contradiction introduced by the change. The modified text directly reverses the original intent, creating a clear and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.changed_text: Regardless of the outcome, each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees in any action to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the allocation of costs and attorneys' fees in the event of a legal dispute. The original clause stipulated that the prevailing party would be entitled to these costs, while the revised clause states that each party must bear its own expenses, irrespective of the outcome. This will directly create in-text contradiction in section {10}.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, such provisions shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that this Agreement shall otherwise remain in full force and effect. This Agreement shall be governed by the law of the State of California without regard to the conflicts of law provisions thereof. This Agreement supersedes all prior discussions and writings and constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Regardless of the outcome, each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees in any action to enforce this Agreement. No waiver or modification of this Agreement will be binding upon either party unless made in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of such party and no failure or delay in enforcing any right will be deemed a waiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the prevailing party is entitled to costs and attorney's fees. The changed text states that regardless of the outcome, each party bears its own costs and attorney's fees. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text contains the changed text within it, directly replacing the original text in the same location, therefore creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' type.
